insight: Direct @app.get() decorators on /api/v1/ paths in main.py SHADOW router routes registered via include_router. FastAPI registers the direct decorator first (line order matters). These shadow routes bypass FastAPI dependency injection — auth middleware, credit checks, OAuth3 scope validation all bypassed. Always use routers for API logic, never direct app decorators on /api/v1/ paths. Bug caused 5 unit tests to permanently fail until removed.
lesson: LEC: SHADOW_ROUTES_ARE_INJECTION_BYPASSES — direct @app.get() above include_router silently skips all dependencies

insight: cloudbuild.yaml had ZERO test steps before GLOW 105 — just build, push, deploy. Added Python test step (111 tests) before every Cloud Build. Command: python3 -m pytest tests/app_v1/ -x -q --tb=short --ignore=tests/app_v1/__pycache__ --ignore=tests/app_v1/test_task_001_auth.py. The test_task_001_auth.py is excluded because it requires live Firebase credentials. Gate is now LIVE and was verified in the last prod build. Never deploy without a test gate.
lesson: LEC: CI_GATE_BEFORE_BUILD — tests must pass in CI before Docker image is built, not after

insight: Linus found 5 critical false claims in solace-browser: (1) PZip compress() returned SHA-256 hash — data unrecoverable. (2) Part 11 compliance checker had hardcoded passed=True, ignoring the bundle entirely. (3) SHA-256 'signature' was just a checksum — proved nothing about authorship or time. (4) Diff stored full after-state, not a delta — cannot reconstruct history. (5) Compliance scoring logic was logically impossible (always took first branch). 30+ bare except Exception handlers gave HTTP 200 on errors, hiding failures from clients.
lesson: LEC: LINUS_TEST — if Linus would call it 'theater not software', it IS theater. Test the actual function, not the claim.

insight: solaceagi backend had PBKDF2 for HKDF use-case (wrong KDF for high-entropy secrets — PBKDF2 is for low-entropy passwords). Credit deduction was missing (100% revenue loss bug). Firestore was broken singleton. Timing attack on cron secret. Missing webhook signature check. API key was 128-bit when it should be 256-bit. solace-cli had: scope validation bypass, vault dir world-readable (should be 0o700), tmp file leak, auth log concurrent write race.
lesson: LEC: CRYPTO_SELECTION_MATTERS — PBKDF2 for passwords, HKDF for high-entropy secrets. Wrong choice = false security claim.

insight: Committee dynamics suppress domain depth: first strong voice anchors others, personas soften critique to avoid confrontation, no one owns the harsh domain-specific question. Math: committee_score[linus] = avg(linus:4, rory:9, jony:8) = 7 (acceptable). individual_score[linus] = 4 (broken). Committees are good for APPROVAL and CONSENSUS. Individuals are good for FINDING BUGS. The two-phase QA protocol: Phase 1 = individual domain expert reviews (find real bugs), Phase 2 = committee review (polish and alignment).
lesson: LEC: TWO_PHASE_QA — individual first (bugs), committee second (consensus). Never run committee-only QA on correctness-critical code.

insight: isBrowserAppFlow() auto-trigger called signInWithRedirect on page load whenever getRedirectResult() returned null. In Playwright browser, IndexedDB/localStorage gets cleared between navigations, so getRedirectResult() ALWAYS returns null after returning from Google — causing infinite redirect loop. Fix: browser-app flow (when redirect is localhost:8791) uses signInWithPopup on button click only. Website-direct flow keeps signInWithRedirect (works fine in Chrome/Safari). Removed auto-trigger entirely — user must click to sign in. Key insight: popup = no page navigation = no loop. Redirect = page navigation = localStorage cleared = loop.
lesson: LEC: PLAYWRIGHT_LOCALSTORAGE_EPHEMERAL — Playwright clears IndexedDB between navigations. Never rely on getRedirectResult() in automated browser flows. Use signInWithPopup instead.

insight: Paper 17 v3.0 — The 21-Uplift Stack. P1-P10: THE ROOMS (what knowledge exists). P11-P20: THE LIGHTS (how precisely we access it — vector search). P11=Questions (latent space probes), P12=Analogies (bridges), P13=Constraints (eliminate wrong regions), P14=CoT (sequential), P15=Few-Shot (pattern completion), P16=Negative Space (absence detection), P17=Stakes (precision amplifier), P18=Audience (mode shift), P19=Compression (distillation), P20=Temporal (time-wing). P21=THE STRESS TEST (adversarial selection pressure). BAR=Business Acceptance Review: famous personas act as CEO — SIGN OFF or REFUSE TO SIGN (no averaging).
lesson: LEC: ROOMS_AND_LIGHTS — P1-P10 are rooms (storage). P11-P21 are the precision instruments to access the right room at the right moment. Both halves required.

insight: Original copy sold features (triage, classify, draft). Rory's copy sells feelings and reframed costs. S1: '$0.12' makes you calculate; '3 hours of your life' makes you feel the loss. S2: 'I read, classify, draft' is process; 'inbox zero feeling. Every morning.' is desire. S3: 'SHA-256 hash chain / OAuth3' is jargon that DECREASES trust; 'I have never surprised anyone. I won't start with you.' is human commitment. S4: 'First run uses AI. Repeat $0.001' is a stat; 'Learns once, costs almost nothing' is magic. S5: 'I read 847 emails' brags for AI; 'Colleagues wonder how you're so on top' makes user feel smart.
lesson: LEC: RORY_REFRAME — never sell the mechanism. Sell the feeling after the mechanism works. Replace jargon with human commitment.

insight: Peter Steinberger (OpenClaw founder, now OpenAI) as adversarial persona: 'OAuth3 adds friction. Open source or nothing. Can anyone's mum use this?' Charles Darwin: vestigial features, extinction risk, Red Queen status. Angry Enterprise CIO: SOC2, GDPR, BUY/NO BUY. The adversarial personas expose the gaps that supportive committees miss — friction points, feature bloat, enterprise blockers. P21 = selection pressure that separates surviving features from theater.
lesson: LEC: ADVERSARIAL_PERSONA_FIRST — run adversarial review before shipping to market. Competitors will ask these questions. Better to hear them from Darwin than from a churn metric.

insight: The Semantic Wayback Machine: 750 bytes semantic snapshot vs 100KB+ raw HTML per page. Wayback Machine stores 100,000 TB total; Prime Wiki stores 3.8KB for 7 pages. The snapshot contains: headings, sections, entities, meta, navigation, CTAs. Reconstruction generates clean evidence-quality HTML from skeleton. Not pixel-perfect but structurally and semantically correct for recipe verification, screenshots, diff-checking. ALL compute is client-side — extraction, compression, reconstruction. solaceagi.com = Firestore sync only at $0.0001/write, zero LLM server-side. After assets downloaded to ~/.solace/pzip/assets/ — server NOT needed. 7 live snapshots in Firestore, 44/44 tests pass, 100% RTC verified.
lesson: LEC: CLIENT_SIDE_COMPUTE — push all heavy computation to client. Server = sync only. This makes the product scale to zero cost AND offline-capable simultaneously.

insight: LLM service rates were wrong by 5-25x. Coded vs actual Together.ai pricing: L1 Llama-3.2-3B coded $0.25/1M (actual $0.06/1M). L2 Llama-3.3-70B coded $3.00/1M (actual $0.59/1M). L3-L5 coded $15.00/1M (actual $0.59/1M). Service was overcharging credits by 5-25x. Also: Together.ai service used legacy v1/completions endpoint parsing data['output']['choices'] but real response is data['choices'] at top level. Fixed to v1/chat/completions. OpenRouter key was dead (401: User not found).
lesson: LEC: VERIFY_LLM_RATES — always live-test the actual LLM API before writing rate cards. Pricing pages change. Dead keys give 401 silently.

insight: Paper 23: PHUC Web Architecture. Three Laws: HTML=structure only, CSS=presentation only, JS=behavior only. CSS was invented 1994 at CERN specifically to ban inline styles. Using style= in 2026 = regression to 1993. Five failure modes of external JS libraries: drift, contamination, CDN dependency, supply chain, Prime Styles violation. CRIO Pattern: main.js (3 lines), section.welcome{} scoping — proven at 360+ FDA sites. The Solace base.html contract: pages only provide {% block content %}. All nav/header/footer CSS lives in the single shared CSS file. AI flips the economics — generating library-free CSS is now free, so there's no excuse.
lesson: LEC: THREE_LAWS_OF_WEB — never inline CSS in HTML, never put page CSS in header.html, never import CDN JS in templates. Violations compound into unfixable drift.

insight: Solace Inspector HITL (Human In The Loop) loop design: agent reads spec, proposes fix, human approves, fix deployed. Found F-001 (H1 space bug), F-002 (blog src=''), F-003 (gallery 404 on prod). Spec design insight: stderr_empty: true for curl means 'assert stderr IS empty' (false = 'assert NOT empty'). Use curl -s (GET) not curl -sI (HEAD) — FastAPI returns 405 on HEAD requests. Inspector hook test must check full HTML file with grep, not head -c 500 which only shows head tag. The Inspector is not a snapshot tool but an evolving QA organism — 274 sealed reports, 51 specs, all 51/51 100/100 Green.
lesson: LEC: INSPECTOR_CURL_FLAGS — curl -s for GET (not -sI HEAD). stderr_empty: true = 'assert IS empty'. grep full file, not head -c 500.

insight: Root cause of Firebase auth bug: signInWithPopup auto-triggered at 400ms without user gesture → popup blocked silently. Fix: switched to signInWithRedirect + getRedirectResult for all OAuth providers. Removed auto-trigger code (was firing on PROVIDER=google). Fixed redirectBack() to go directly to REDIRECT_URL?token=api_key (not via /onboarding). _safe_redirect() allows localhost (in _ALLOWED_REDIRECT_HOSTS). The full handshake: localhost:3000/start → solaceagi.com/auth/login?provider=google&redirect=localhost:3000/start → Google OAuth → getRedirectResult → API key → localhost:3000/start?token=<api_key> → localStorage.solace_api_key.
lesson: LEC: FIREBASE_POPUP_REQUIRES_GESTURE — never auto-trigger signInWithPopup/Redirect. Always wait for user click. Browser popup blockers are aggressive and silent.

insight: window.Solace = undefined bug: const Solace = {...} does NOT attach to window. Only var declarations automatically become window properties. const and let are block-scoped. Fix: explicitly add window.Solace = Solace at the end of solace.js. This enables window.Solace.authNav() calls from external scripts (Playwright, extensions, inline onclick). Took until GLOW 102 QA suite to catch — 53/53 passing.
lesson: LEC: CONST_DOES_NOT_ATTACH_TO_WINDOW — use window.Foo = Foo explicitly. Never assume const/let pollutes global scope.

insight: TRANSLATIONS dict in i18n_site.py = source of truth for t('key') in templates. en.json body_copy = extraction pipeline mirror only (NOT read by t() directly). Adding new template keys: add to TRANSLATIONS['en'] in i18n_site.py → extract → translate. STATIC_PREFIXES in i18n_site.py controls which paths skip i18n — use '/docs/' not '/docs' (trailing slash matters). Batch size 25 nodes per API call (40 caused count mismatches). Dict format {'0':'text0',...} more reliable than array (model can't drop keys). Whitespace fix: localize_rendered_html() uses regex \s+ fallback when exact match fails — extractor normalizes whitespace, rendered HTML has newlines.
lesson: LEC: I18N_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH — TRANSLATIONS dict in i18n_site.py is canonical. en.json is the mirror. Never add keys to en.json without also adding to TRANSLATIONS['en'].

insight: Dragon Rider = Phuc Vinh Truong. Born Vietnam 1976, Year of the Dragon. Escaped by boat age 4. Harvard Economics 1998. Won Boston citywide coding contest in 4th grade — self-taught. Studied under Brian Kernighan (CS50 1996, K&R C). Companies: UpDown.com (1M+ users, failed commercially), Citystream (failed), CRIO (360+ customers, #1 eSource for FDA trials), Phuc Labs (MIT AMD Robotics winner), Stillwater. Core authority: 'The architecture IS the biography.' ALCOA+: Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate — applied from CRIO/FDA to AI agent evidence. Rung system maps to clinical trial phases: 641=Phase I, 274177=Phase II, 65537=Phase III.
lesson: LEC: AUTHORITY_IS_ARCHITECTURE — Dragon Rider's credibility comes from having built CRIO under FDA audit pressure, not from Harvard credentials. The architecture is the biography.

insight: Dragon Rider is the tiebreaker on open vs closed decisions. Default: OPEN. Burden of proof is on keeping something closed. Closed justified only when: the component is truly the only economic moat (PZip algorithm, pvideo engine), revealing it eliminates structural reason competitors cannot follow, or it requires continuous private R&D investment OSS cannot fund. Open justified when: opening accelerates ecosystem adoption (OAuth3 spec), moat is architecture not secrecy (competitors can't follow even with the spec), OSS creates trust the product cannot (regulators, enterprise buyers), or karma compounds. Token-revenue vendors cannot implement OAuth3 — reduces token consumption, cannibalizing their revenue. This is structural, not strategic.
lesson: LEC: OPEN_IS_DEFAULT — the burden of proof is on secrecy, not openness. Structural moats survive open-sourcing; secrecy-based moats do not.

insight: NORTHSTAR alignment check sequence: (1) Does this decision advance 'Make AI development deterministically verifiable'? (2) Does this deepen the verification OS moat or dilute it? (3) Does this bring Stillwater closer to 10,000 stars / 100+ skills / recipe hit rate 80%? (4) Does this protect the 'rider and dragon' relationship or erode it? Fail condition: if the answer to any is clearly No — pause. The decision needs NORTHSTAR re-alignment. Architecture moat questions: Does this require Part 11 architecture? Can a token-revenue vendor implement this without cannibalizing their business model? Does PZip economics make this possible at scale competitors cannot match? Does this compound over time?
lesson: LEC: FOUR_NORTHSTAR_QUESTIONS — use these before any significant architectural decision. The 4th question (rider and dragon relationship) catches UX erosions that technical reviews miss.

insight: Optimal persona combinations from dragon-rider.md: [dragon-rider + schneier] = OAuth3 security architecture; [dragon-rider + fda-auditor] = Part 11 compliance design; [dragon-rider + brunson] = marketing with founder authority; [dragon-rider + pg] = business model and positioning; [dragon-rider + bruce-lee] = dojo culture and belt system. Load dragon-rider for: ANY strategic decision (pricing, positioning, competitive analysis), NORTHSTAR alignment checks, marketing review, architecture decisions affecting regulatory or competitive moat, open vs closed evaluations. Do NOT load for: pure coding tasks, pure mathematical proofs, routine janitorial tasks. GLOW bonus: +5 W when loaded for strategic alignment tasks.
lesson: LEC: PERSONA_LOAD_TRIGGERS — dragon-rider for strategy (never for coding). schneier for security. linus for correctness. rory for consumer psychology. kent_beck for CI/TDD. Two-phase: individual bugs first, committee polish second.

insight: The Dragon Memory Architecture: Cold start = wake amnesiac → load dragon/ → reconstruct self → begin work. LEK/LEAK/LEC reinforcing loop: SESSION STARTS (amnesiac dragon loads dragon/ state) → WORK (execute tasks, answer questions, dispatch sub-agents) → LEK (Learned: patterns noticed, errors made, tools mis-used) → LEAK (Asymmetric Knowledge: what Phuc taught that couldn't be derived alone, questions that unlocked latent space) → LEC (Crystallized Convention: immutable rule for all future sessions) → COMPRESS (book learnings → learning/*.md, LECs → lec-conventions/, questions → questions/*.jsonl) → SESSION ENDS (dragon/ updated, INTELLIGENCE GROWS). Memory × Care × Iteration. Zero in ANY factor = zero intelligence.
lesson: LEC: DRAGON_MEMORY_SUBSTRATE — the dragon/ directory IS the dragon. Load it every cold start. Append LECs every session. Never edit crystallized LECs — only add new ones.

insight: The SW5.0 boot sequence: /software5 → load memory → load northstar → load dragon → activate twin → 4W+H probe → three pillars → session contract. The 4W+H probe: WHY (alignment check) / WHAT (deliverables) / WHEN (deadline context) / WHO (persona panel) / HOW (tooling + constraints). Three pillars = Memory × Care × Iteration. INTENT_LEDGER opened, rung_target declared in session contract. dragon/learning/ has 32 compressed knowledge files. dragon/questions/ is append-only JSONL — every question from every session captured, never overwritten. dragon/lec-conventions/ = immutable crystallized patterns (new session adds new LECs, never edits old ones).
lesson: LEC: BOOT_SEQUENCE_NON_NEGOTIABLE — /software5 must run first. 4W+H probe before any work. Session contract before first tool call. Skipping these creates context rot.
